Skip to contents
tumr 0.6.1
  • Get started
    • Bayesian Hierarchical Linear Model
    • tumr data sets
    • Troubleshooting
  • Reference

Generalized Additive Model for Tumor Growth Data

Source: R/gam.R
gamFit.Rd

Generalized Additive Model for Tumor Growth Data

Usage

gamFit(
  tumr_obj = NULL,
  formula = NULL,
  data = NULL,
  id = NULL,
  time = NULL,
  measure = NULL,
  group = NULL,
  n_grid = 20,
  ...
)

Arguments

tumr_obj

A tumr object created by tumr.

formula

GAM formula. Defaults to log(measure + 1) ~ group + s(time, by = group) + s(id, bs = "re") + s(id, time, bs = "re").

data

A data frame of tumor growth measurements.

id

Column name of subject IDs.

time

Column name of repeated time measurements.

measure

Column name of repeated tumor measurements.

group

Column name of treatment group assignments.

n_grid

Number of time grid points for prediction. Default 20.

...

Further arguments passed to gam.

Value

An object of class "tumr_gam" with components:

fit

The mgcv::gam object.

data

Processed data with internal column names.

formula

The formula used.

relevant_info

Named list of column names and reference group.

n_grid

Number of prediction grid points.

Examples

data(melanoma1)
mel1 <- tumr(melanoma1, ID, Day, Volume, Treatment)
gamFit(mel1)
#> $fit
#> 
#> Family: gaussian 
#> Link function: identity 
#> 
#> Formula:
#> log(.measure + 1) ~ .group + s(.time, by = .group) + s(.id, bs = "re") + 
#>     s(.id, .time, bs = "re")
#> 
#> Estimated degrees of freedom:
#>  1.63  5.93  3.96  6.67 15.46 28.73  total = 66.37 
#> 
#> REML score: 775.6923     
#> 
#> $data
#>     ID Day     Volume Treatment .id .time   .measure .group
#> 1    1   0   44.10900         A   1     0   44.10900      A
#> 2    1   3   57.15400         A   1     3   57.15400      A
#> 3    1   6   65.82400         A   1     6   65.82400      A
#> 4    1  16   64.00000         A   1    16   64.00000      A
#> 5    1  24  126.40500         A   1    24  126.40500      A
#> 6    1  34  355.20000         A   1    34  355.20000      A
#> 7    1  38  380.46938         A   1    38  380.46938      A
#> 8    1  41  396.80000         A   1    41  396.80000      A
#> 9    1  45  803.63175         A   1    45  803.63175      A
#> 10   1  53 1115.40000         A   1    53 1115.40000      A
#> 11   1  58 1273.60800         A   1    58 1273.60800      A
#> 12   1  63 2013.75000         A   1    63 2013.75000      A
#> 13   2   0   46.22500         A   2     0   46.22500      A
#> 14   2   3   45.38700         A   2     3   45.38700      A
#> 15   2   6   61.85200         A   2     6   61.85200      A
#> 16   2  16  142.72100         A   2    16  142.72100      A
#> 17   2  24  277.24800         A   2    24  277.24800      A
#> 18   2  34  480.63150         A   2    34  480.63150      A
#> 19   2  38  480.12800         A   2    38  480.12800      A
#> 20   2  41  438.46376         A   2    41  438.46376      A
#> 21   2  45 1289.06250         A   2    45 1289.06250      A
#> 22   2  53  869.93800         A   2    53  869.93800      A

#> [1] "tumr_gam"

On this page

Developed by Patrick Breheny, Sydney Plowman, Tian Zhao.

Last updated: June 15, 2026
